title.purpose: Cards, panels, and selected semantic objects.

label.purpose: Modes, states, compact metadata, and proof markers.

shadows.name: floating-panel
shadows.purpose: Temporary discovery panels and the Semantic Passport.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Compact state, border, and background feedback.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Node focus, detail disclosure, and camera feedback.

components.name: Toolbar Button
components.description: Compact desktop control with familiar text and a visible focus ring.
components.css: .ds-toolbar-button { min-height: 2.75rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.875rem; border: 1px solid #334155; border-radius: 0.5rem; background: rgba(15,23,42,0.8); color: #e2e8f0; font: 500 0.75rem/1.4 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; transition: background 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-toolbar-button:hover { background: rgba(15,23,42,0.95); border-color: #64748b; } .ds-toolbar-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Diagram Panel
components.css: .ds-diagram-panel { display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; min-width: 18rem; padding: 1.5rem; border: 1px solid #1e293b; border-radius: 1rem; background: rgba(15,23,42,0.5); color: #fff; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-diagram-panel small { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-diagram-panel:hover { border-color: #334155; } .ds-diagram-panel:focus-within { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Node Finder Field
components.description: One continuous technical search field with no decorative split controls.
components.html: <input class="ds-search-field" placeholder="Search labels or IDs" aria-label="Search labels or IDs">
components.css: .ds-search-field { width: 20rem; padding: 0.58rem 0.65rem; border: 1px solid #334155; border-radius: 0.5rem; background: #0f172a; color: #fff; font: 400 0.75rem/1.4 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; transition: border-color 160ms ease, box-shadow 160ms ease; } .ds-search-field::placeholder { color: #7d8da1; } .ds-search-field:hover { border-color: #64748b; } .ds-search-field:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #22d3ee; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(34,211,238,0.2); }

components.name: Semantic Passport
components.description: One focused node's compact metadata, authored reachability, relationships, and optional verified sources.
components.css: .ds-passport { display: grid; gap: 0.4rem; width: 18rem; padding: 0.75rem; border: 1px solid #22d3ee; border-radius: 0.82rem; background: #0f172a; color: #fff; box-shadow: 0 18px 48px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-passport span { color: #22d3ee; font-size: 0.5rem; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.14em; } .ds-passport small { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-passport:focus-within { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Authored Reachability
components.description: Two compact Semantic Passport actions that reveal the deterministic upstream or downstream closure of authored relationships without claiming runtime causality.
components.html: 9 nodes · 9 links · max 6 hops
components.css: .ds-reach { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.38rem; width: 18rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-reach button { min-height: 2rem; border: 1px solid #334155; border-radius: 0.45rem; background: #0f172a; color: #cbd5e1; font: 700 0.65rem/1 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-reach button[aria-pressed="true"] { border-color: #22d3ee; color: #22d3ee; background: rgba(34,211,238,0.08); } .ds-reach b { color: inherit; } .ds-reach small { grid-column: 1 / -1; color: #94a3b8; font-size: 0.6rem; } .ds-reach button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Reach Share Card
components.description: An explicit 1200 by 630 authored upstream or downstream reading that preserves full-graph context without claiming runtime impact.
components.html: ARCHIFY · DOWNSTREAM REACHAuthored downstream from Command Router9 nodes · 9 links · max 6 hops
components.css: .ds-reach-card { display: grid; gap: 0.55rem; width: 24rem; padding: 1rem 1.1rem; border: 1px solid #334155; border-left: 3px solid #34d399; border-radius: 0.65rem; background: #020617; color: #fff; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-reach-card span { color: #34d399; font-size: 0.52rem; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.13em; } .ds-reach-card strong { font-size: 0.85rem; } .ds-reach-card small { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-reach-card i { height: 2px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #34d399 0 58%, #1e293b 58%); } .ds-reach-card[data-direction="upstream"] { border-left-color: #a78bfa; } .ds-reach-card[data-direction="upstream"] span { color: #a78bfa; }

components.name: Verified Source Beacon
components.description: A quiet viewer-only count that identifies evidence-backed nodes.
components.css: .ds-evidence-beacon { display: inline-grid; place-items: center; width: 30px; height: 12px; border: 1px solid #34d399; border-radius: 999px; background: #0f172a; color: #34d399; font: 800 5.5px/1 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; letter-spacing: 0.06em; transition: opacity 160ms ease, filter 160ms ease; } .ds-evidence-beacon:hover { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px rgba(52,211,153,0.72)); } .ds-evidence-beacon:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: Archify is a composed technical instrument, not a drawing suite. The canvas carries one spatial narrative; restrained controls and progressive disclosure let a reader move from the primary path to exact authored relationships, metadata, and verified code evidence without losing orientation. The visual system is precise, dark-first, and vivid only where semantics earn it. Light mode and Blueprint preserve the same vocabulary rather than becoming separate products. Motion has one bounded owner, finishes, and never carries meaning that disappears in a still frame. Desktop is the primary surface; narrow screens receive containment, not a second interface.
keyCharacteristics: One dominant technical canvas with compact, low-interference chrome.
A fixed semantic color vocabulary shared by nodes, edges, legends, focus, and evidence.
Mono-forward typography, small labels, and deliberate density for engineering review.
Viewer state stays outside canonical exports; proof remains portable and deterministic.
Authored reachability is explicit, finite, shareable, and never presented as runtime impact.
Scoped share variants name their authored state, direction, origin, counts, and clean-state proof.
State transitions are 140–200ms; authored story motion may be longer but finite and reader-controlled.

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Every saturated color maps to a node or relationship meaning; never add an accent merely to make a surface lively.

rules.name: The Theme Parity Rule
rules.body: Light, dark, Signal Flow, and Blueprint may change material and contrast, but must preserve category identity and information priority.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: UI and diagram chrome remain mono-forward; never introduce a display font inside the generated artifact.

rules.name: The Legibility Floor Rule
rules.body: Tiny labels are metadata, not prose. If a reader must parse a sentence, promote it to body size or disclose it on focus.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: A static card or control uses border and tone; a shadow must explain layering or state.

rules.name: The Canonical Clean Rule
rules.body: Viewer glow, overlays, focus, and temporary marks are removed from visual exports unless the export explicitly owns that narrative.

dos: Do make the primary path legible before adding secondary relationships or detail.
Do derive every focus, route, story, source link, receipt, and count from authored or locally verified evidence.
Do label upstream and downstream exploration as authored reachability, deduplicate relationship fragments, terminate cycles, and preserve deterministic DOM order.
Do name the authored scope, direction, origin, node count, link count, and maximum hops on every Reach Share Card.
Do keep ordinary artifacts source-free and make repository evidence explicitly opt-in.
Do use 140–200ms transitions for control state and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
Do preserve keyboard access, visible focus, semantic labels, dark/light parity, and non-color state cues.
Do keep runtime exploration outside canonical SVG, raster, Share Card, and WebM backgrounds.
donts: Don't build generic Mermaid beautifiers that change themes without improving information architecture.
Don't turn Archify into WYSIWYG drawing suites whose editing chrome becomes the product.
Don't ship motion-first graph demos that imply relationships or activity not present in the authored source.
Don't call authored graph reachability blast radius, breakage, dependency impact, or runtime causality.
Don't present a Reach Share Card as proof of impact, failure propagation, breakage, or runtime causality.
Don't use dense dashboard shells, endless identical card grids, decorative glass, gradient text, and other AI-generated interface clichés.
Don't add vendor-icon catalogues or product-name matching that weaken Archify's portable semantic vocabulary.
Don't create another permanent panel when the Semantic Passport, Node Finder, or existing canvas can carry the capability.
Don't change the README Hero as a side effect of artifact or viewer iteration.
